_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 6-3 Pediatric Tachycardia (Revised 11/17/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Pediatric Cardiac Protocol Section 6-3: Pediatric Tachycardia — MDHHS Approved 11/17/25, Reviewed 2025
purpose: PARAMEDIC USE ONLY. Intended for symptomatic pediatric patients with elevated heart rate relative to their age. Refer to MI-MEDIC for appropriate vital signs and medication doses.

sections.title: General Assessment and Initial Treatment
points: PARAMEDIC USE ONLY
Follow General Pre-Hospital Care-Treatment Protocol
Determine if patient is stable or unstable
Manage airway as necessary
Provide supplemental oxygen as needed to maintain SpO2 > 94%
Initiate monitoring
Perform 12-lead EKG — do NOT delay care for 12-lead EKG on unstable patients
Establish vascular access
Pediatric patients (< 14 years) utilize MI-MEDIC cards for appropriate medication dosage — when unavailable utilize pediatric dosing listed within protocol
Identify and treat UNDERLYING causes: dehydration, fever, vomiting, sepsis, pain
Administer NS or LR bolus 20 mL/kg with possible hypovolemia

sections.title: UNSTABLE — Regular Narrow Complex Tachycardia
points: Prepare for IMMEDIATE cardioversion — in conscious patients consider sedation per Patient Procedural Sedation-Procedure Protocol
Perform synchronized cardioversion 1 J/kg
Increase dose to 2 J/kg for additional synchronized cardioversion if needed
DO NOT EXCEED ADULT DOSING
If able to convert: maintain full cardiac monitoring including pulse oximetry and supportive care until transfer of care at receiving facility

sections.title: UNSTABLE — Regular Wide Complex Tachycardia
points: Prepare for IMMEDIATE cardioversion — in conscious patients consider sedation per Patient Procedural Sedation-Procedure Protocol
Perform synchronized cardioversion 1 J/kg
For recurrent or refractory wide complex unstable tachycardia: consult Medical Control PRIOR to medication administration (medication per MCA selection — amiodarone or lidocaine per MI-MEDIC dosing)
NOTE: MI-MEDIC includes amiodarone for shock-resistant V-fib — dosage is the SAME for unstable wide complex tachycardia with a pulse, administered slow IV push
If able to convert: maintain full cardiac monitoring and supportive care until transfer

sections.title: UNSTABLE — Irregular Wide Complex Tachycardia
points: Defibrillate according to Electrical Therapy-Procedure Protocol
Refer to Pediatric General Cardiac Arrest Protocol

sections.title: STABLE — Regular Narrow Complex Tachycardia (SVT)
points: Perform vagal maneuvers — ensure patient is on oxygen and cardiac monitor; run ECG strip during procedure; DO NOT USE CAROTID MASSAGE
If child IS ABLE to follow instructions: (a) blow into a 10 mL syringe for 15 seconds; (b) squat and bear down

If child is NOT ABLE to follow instructions: (a) while supine, elevate patient's legs to knee-chest position for 60 seconds; (b) if available, consider QUICKLY placing a bag of ice on the eyes and forehead — do NOT occlude the nose or place below the bridge of the nose; results generally seen within 15 seconds; this is an ABRUPT maneuver — do NOT maintain for more than 15 seconds
Contact Medical Control PRIOR to adenosine administration

Administer adenosine per MI-MEDIC cards if vagal maneuvers are ineffective; if MI-MEDIC not available: 0.1 mg/kg (maximum 6 mg) rapid IV push through most proximal injection site, immediately followed by 10 mL flush; may repeat ONCE with 0.2 mg/kg (maximum 12 mg) using same technique

sections.title: STABLE — Regular Wide Complex Monomorphic QRS Tachycardia
points: Contact Medical Control
Consider adenosine per MI-MEDIC cards; if MI-MEDIC not available: 0.1 mg/kg (maximum 6 mg) rapid IV push through most proximal injection site, immediately followed by 10 mL flush
May repeat ONCE with 0.2 mg/kg (maximum 12 mg) using same technique

mca_selections_required.item: Antidysrhythmic — Refractory/Recurrent Wide Complex Unstable Tachycardia
mca_selections_required.description: MCA must select antidysrhythmic for refractory or recurrent wide complex unstable tachycardia (with Medical Control contact required prior to administration). Use MI-MEDIC for dosing.
options: Amiodarone 5 mg/kg IV/IO slow push over 10 minutes (max single dose 300 mg; may repeat twice; total max 450 mg IV/IO)
Lidocaine 1 mg/kg IV/IO (may repeat 0.5 mg/kg twice at 5–10 minute intervals; maximum 3 doses total)

key_drugs.name: Adenosine IV (Pediatric SVT — First Dose)
key_drugs.dose: Per MI-MEDIC cards; backup: 0.1 mg/kg (maximum 6 mg) rapid IV push through most proximal injection site, immediately followed by 10 mL flush
key_drugs.route: IV (rapid push at most proximal injection site + 10 mL flush)
key_drugs.indication: Stable SVT not converting with vagal maneuvers — requires Medical Control contact PRIOR to administration
key_drugs.contraindications: Irregular rhythms; Wolff-Parkinson-White with A-Fib; contact Medical Control before administration
key_drugs.side_effects: Transient asystole, flushing, chest pressure, dyspnea
key_drugs.notes: Use MI-MEDIC for weight-based dosing. MUST contact Medical Control before administration. Rapid push technique with immediate 10 mL flush.

key_drugs.name: Adenosine IV (Pediatric SVT — Second Dose)
key_drugs.dose: Per MI-MEDIC cards; backup: 0.2 mg/kg (maximum 12 mg) rapid IV push + 10 mL flush
key_drugs.route: IV (rapid push at most proximal injection site + 10 mL flush)
key_drugs.indication: SVT not converting after first adenosine dose
key_drugs.side_effects: Transient asystole, flushing, chest pressure, dyspnea
key_drugs.notes: Use MI-MEDIC when available. Same rapid push technique.

key_drugs.name: Amiodarone IV/IO (Pediatric Refractory Wide Complex)
key_drugs.dose: 5 mg/kg IV/IO slow push over 10 minutes (max single dose 300 mg) — may repeat twice; total maximum 450 mg IV/IO; per MI-MEDIC when available
key_drugs.route: IV or IO (slow push over 10 minutes)
key_drugs.indication: Refractory or recurrent wide complex unstable tachycardia — requires Medical Control contact prior; per MCA selection
key_drugs.contraindications: Must consult Medical Control before administration in refractory/recurrent wide complex
key_drugs.scope: Paramedic — per MCA selection with Medical Control contact
key_drugs.notes: NOTE: MI-MEDIC dosage for amiodarone in shock-resistant V-fib is the SAME dose used for unstable wide complex tachycardia with a pulse — administer as slow IV push.

key_drugs.name: Lidocaine IV/IO (Pediatric Wide Complex)
key_drugs.dose: 1 mg/kg IV/IO — may repeat 0.5 mg/kg twice at 5–10 minute intervals; maximum 3 doses total; per MI-MEDIC when available
key_drugs.indication: Refractory wide complex unstable tachycardia — per MCA selection with Medical Control contact
key_drugs.contraindications: Must consult Medical Control before administration
key_drugs.scope: Paramedic — per MCA selection with Medical Control contact
key_drugs.notes: Use MI-MEDIC for dosing. Maximum 3 doses total.

deterioration_flags: Stable patient becomes unstable (hypotension, AMS, shock signs) — immediate synchronized cardioversion; consider sedation
Irregular wide complex (Torsades or A-fib with aberrancy) — DEFIBRILLATE (unsynchronized); refer to Pediatric Cardiac Arrest protocol
Wide complex regular tachycardia (stable) — contact Medical Control; consider adenosine before antidysrhythmic
Cardioversion failure at maximum energy — contact Medical Control for antidysrhythmic per MCA selection
SVT with adenosine failure — contact Medical Control before repeat adenosine dose
scope_notes: This protocol is PARAMEDIC USE ONLY
Adenosine IV: Paramedic — Medical Control contact REQUIRED before administration for stable SVT
Amiodarone, lidocaine: Paramedic — per MCA selection; Medical Control contact required for refractory wide complex
Refer to MI-MEDIC for all age-appropriate vital sign ranges and medication doses
